nodes.title: Nara Period Begins
nodes.description: Heijo-kyo (Nara) is established as the permanent capital, starting a cultural peak.

nodes.title: Heian Period
nodes.description: The capital moves to Heian-kyo (Kyoto), initiating a classical golden age of art and literature.

nodes.title: Kamakura Shogunate
nodes.description: Minamoto no Yoritomo establishes the military dictatorship, beginning the feudal era of shoguns.

nodes.title: Sengoku Period
nodes.description: The Onin War triggers a century of civil war and social upheaval among warlords.

nodes.title: Tokugawa Shogunate
nodes.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu unifies Japan, establishing the Edo period of long peace and isolation.

nodes.title: Meiji Restoration
nodes.description: Imperial rule is restored, ending the shogunates and launching rapid westernized modernization.

nodes.title: Showa Era Begins
nodes.description: Hirohito becomes Emperor, marking an era covering military expansionism and later democratic recovery.

nodes.title: Economic Miracle
nodes.description: Post-war Japan achieves rapid industrial and technological growth to become a global economy.
